name: IFTTT
short_description: A web automation platform that connects apps, services, and smart devices through conditional 'if this, then that' Applets.
long_description: IFTTT (short for 'If This Then That') lets people wire together over a thousand services and connected devices using simple trigger-action rules called Applets, so an event on one service automatically causes an action on another. For physical AI in daily life it acts as the connective tissue between voice assistants, smart-home gear, sensors, and the cloud, turning isolated devices into coordinated routines without code. By exposing triggers (a door opening, a location entered, a phrase spoken to Alexa or Google Assistant) and actions across an open ecosystem, it shaped how everyday people compose ambient, situated automations in the home.

images.caption: The IFTTT wordmark logo.
images.alt_text: The IFTTT logo, lowercase black letters spelling 'ifttt' on a white background.

sources.title: What is IFTTT and how does it work?
sources.access_notes: Official explainer describing Applets, triggers, and actions.

sources.title: IFTTT
sources.access_notes: Founding history (launched September 7, 2011; founders Linden Tibbets et al.) and platform description.

interactions.title: Lights waiting when you pull in
interactions.description: Your phone crossing the geofence around the house tells the Hue bulbs to switch on, so the porch and hallway are already lit as you walk in after dark.

interactions.title: The lamp blinks when the doorbell rings
interactions.description: Whenever someone presses the Ring doorbell, an Applet makes the LIFX bulbs flash a chosen color across the room, so a visitor registers even with headphones on or the TV loud.
interactions.source_label: IFTTT — flash lights when Ring rings

interactions.title: Every rainfall logged to a spreadsheet
interactions.description: When the local weather condition flips to rain, a row lands in a Google Sheet with the time, temperature, and forecast link, quietly building a personal weather diary nobody has to maintain by hand.
interactions.source_label: IFTTT — log rain to a spreadsheet

interactions.title: A nudge to step outside for the space station
interactions.description: Enthusiasts wire a polling trigger to fire when the ISS is about to pass over their exact location, sending a phone notification minutes before so they can go outside and catch it overhead.

interactions.title: A library of 200,000 homemade automations
interactions.description: Researchers cataloguing 224,590 publicly shared Applets found over 100,000 ordinary people composing trigger-action rules with no code, evidence of how widely non-programmers reached for this to stitch their own ambient routines together.
interactions.source_label: Ur et al., CHI 2016 — Trigger-Action Programming in the Wild

interactions.title: A panic button on the home screen
interactions.description: One tap of the Button widget fires an alarm to a chosen safety service or sends a preset SMS with your location to a trusted contact, turning a phone-screen tile into a discreet call for help.

interactions.title: Say one phrase, run a whole scene
interactions.description: A single spoken command to Alexa or Google Assistant could fan out into a coordinated good-night routine — locking the door, dimming the lights, and dropping the thermostat — by chaining the voice trigger to several device actions IFTTT already brokers.
